inline_comments.message: Better to add at least few words explaining the purpose of the class.
why do you need setter & constructor with fields both?
Same comment. why you need constructor with fields & setter both?
you don't need these methods. Just use Getter to get the list and then add/remove from the returned list.

suppose these generated policies are ONLY used for OOF, whose policyType is static/defined as the specified type.

since it will be used in both decode and extractInfo functions.
Please add a period at the end of the first sentence of Javadoc comments or checkstyle will complain. Please propagate this comment throughout the other classes in the review.

Java prefers camel case rather than c style for variable names, this variable should be called attributeName.

Please propagate this change to other variable names in the class and also to the setter and getter method names.
Just a question, what's the significance of "a" and "b"?
There should be a space before the parenthesis. Do you have your checkstyle plugin turned on in your IDE? It would catch these things.
This should be a Javadoc comment, this is a public method.
Again, although this is a provate method, use Javadoc format for the comment.
Comments again. Please propagate to rest of class.

Liam, it is used to generate the policy by Gson, whose format is defined in https://wiki.onap.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=40206714 
the attribute_name is regarded as a keyword in the OOF/MultiCloud/AAI, that's why use this kind instead of camel case
just a placeholder, which to be changed once we define which value should be added. i'll remove them
I think the pom checkstyle doesn't find everything. You need to run it in your IDE as a plugin.
That's ok. But still you should pass exception object to logger.

That file is defined to only have Optimization policies so it is ok there. But I think this class is more generic and can represent any type of Pdpx policy including optimization. Having it static will not let that happen.
agree that it is used in 2 methods but they both belong to same flow. So you can take return from one method and pass it to next method.
ok. i'll change to make it as the default value instead of static.
ok. i'll do it. one variable is ok to return/pass, once there are more than one, it may become NOT convenient.
